title: Dashboard Filters Applied without notice
excerpt:
Has anyone noticed their dashboards reporting strangely due to ‘dashboard filters’ being applied without notice? 
I can’t find anything out about what this is and why it was applied without my knowledge. There is no togg…

posts.author: Lyndsay_Q (Lyndsay Quilter)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has anyone noticed their dashboards reporting strangely due to ‘dashboard filters’ being applied without notice?

I can’t find anything out about what this is and why it was applied without my knowledge. There is no toggle to remove it other than having to go into each individual widget and deselect it.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are there other owners of the dashboard? Could someone else have changed the filter?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Lyndsay_Q (Lyndsay Quilter)
posts.cookedHtml: I figured out how these filters are being applied - the issue is probably more with the dashboard functionality itself. I am having difficulty getting the dashboards to report correctly. I will get my widgets configured the way they need to be, but when I try to use the dashboard filters at the top, the application of that filter to the widgets makes it report in the strangest way. I lose a lot of data that should be present.
